sessions.notes: Cheat-asm removal pass. HEAD has 3 cheat-asm constructs (a1val pin, arg0 pin + barrier, asm volatile barrier — see candidate.c header). HEAD byte-matches via these cheats. Stripping the cheats holds sandbox masked-score at 1, but the residual is a 2-instruction branch-sense flip GCC's branch-inversion peephole imposes. Original commit message (9490fd3a) explicitly identifies the barrier as defeating this peephole.

levers_tested.form: drop a1 pin + a0 pin/barrier + asm volatile barrier only
inverted outer if: test (val != dir) first
do { } while(a1val != 0) loop instead of goto loop
move done: label before next: in source order
levers_tested.verdict: no effect — label position doesn't drive BB ordering here

levers_tested.form: explicit goto loop_end + done: return + next: loop-end

sessions.notes: Round-4 worker. Worktree HEAD was f36c9fe1 (64 commits stale, predating the WIP infrastructure). Reset --hard main; setup_worktree.ps1 bootstrapped junctions (.venv/build/gcc-2.7.2/decomp-permuter/disc). Re-verified candidate.c floor=1 via sandbox cheat-stripped + tmp/diff_csmdt.py: confirmed 2-byte residual at masked insns 48 (bnez vs beqz) and 50 (j target swap), exactly as documented. Tried 3 additional novel structural levers; all converged on the identical insn-48/50 branch-sense flip; no new floor below 1. Read tools/gcc-2.7.2/jump.c L1737-1787 (cond-jump-over-uncond-jump invert peephole) and reorg.c mostly_true_jump L1335-1424 to verify the inversion choice originates at do_jump/jumpif lowering (expr.c) — far before reorg sees it. The reorg.c prediction logic (NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG path) explains why session-1's do/while regressed (loop note shifts prediction) but the residual choice in our candidate.c is already past that path. NOT executed (genuine remaining avenues): (a) permuter from candidate.c base (requires extracting single-function target.o + base.c + compile.sh — multi-step setup), (b) instrumented cc1 with BB2_JUMP_DEBUG hooks added to jump.c's invert_jump (would build separate tmp/gccdbg/cc1, canonical untouched), (c) Kengo PS2 source cross-reference for nm_cpu/cpu_check_same_dir_timer.

levers_tested.form: inner-inversion: `if (dir >= val) goto next; goto done;` (swap which goto is conditional)
levers_tested.verdict: no effect — GCC canonicalizes >= and < to identical RTL post-combine

levers_tested.form: structured nested if/else-if: `if (val==dir) {...} else if (dir<val) {goto done;} goto next;`
levers_tested.verdict: no effect — collapses to same RTL/CFG as the 2-goto form

levers_tested.form: while(1) structured loop replacing the goto-loop
levers_tested.verdict: regresses — entry-test prologue insns added; same shape as session-1's do/while regression

sessions.notes: DECISIVE diagnosis session — confirmed the residual is the reorg.c relax_delay_slots branch-sense swap. Walked cc1's RTL dumps via `-da` (tmp/rtl/code6cac_b.i.{rtl,jump,cse,loop,cse2,flow,combine,sched,lreg,greg,jump2,sched2,dbr}); see tmp/rtl_extract.py for the per-pass jump-insn extractor. Found jump_insn 130 stable as `(ne v0 0) target=L168(done)` through sched2; the SAME insn becomes `(eq v0 0) target=L146(next)` in dbr — and jump_insn 138's target swaps inversely. The flip is from reorg.c lines 3829-3851 (the unconditional-jump-after-conditional invert peephole). Confirmed via reorg.c source read that `mostly_true_jump` returns 1 for the `(ne v0 0)` condition via the `case NE: return 1` path at line 1407 (heuristic comment: 'NE tests are usually true'), which satisfies the swap precondition `0 < mostly_true_jump(...)`. Also confirmed via cc1psx parity test (tools/cc1psx_wrapper.sh on tmp/rtl/code6cac_b.i, output at tmp/rtl/code6cac_b.psx.s lines 70-100) that cc1psx produces IDENTICAL swap from candidate.c — this is NOT a fork divergence; target was compiled from a different C source. Then ran the permuter (permuter/csmdt/ setup: target.s = prelude.inc + asm/funcs/cpu_check_same_dir_timer.s with .set gp=64 removed; base.c = preprocessed src/code6cac_b.c + strip_other_fns.py; compile.sh copied from permuter/dbe4/). 3-minute 4-thread run; permuter found a SCORE 0 candidate at permuter/csmdt/output-0-1/ — wrapping the dispatch block in `do { ... } while (0);`. The do-while-0 triggers NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG which makes loop.c set LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P on `done:` (outside the do-while), which makes mostly_true_jump return -1 at reorg.c:1349 instead of reaching the NE-returns-1 fallback, which suppresses the swap. Cheat-reviewer verdict 2026-06-04: FAIL — non-empty-body do-while-0 wrap has no semantic purpose, would not be written by a human, justification cites pure GCC internals, same intent as the original asm volatile("") barrier. Form preserved at memory/wip/cpu_check_same_dir_timer/rejected/do_while_zero_wrapping.c with full mechanism + reviewer evidence in the header comment. RESULT: floor unchanged at 1 / 2 byte diffs. The reorg.c swap mechanism is now FULLY DIAGNOSED at the GCC-source level; the closing form requires either (a) a real loop construct that doesn't trigger LICM hoist (untested — both do-while(a1val != 0) and while(1) regress via LICM hoisting `li v0, 0xFF` and `li v0, 1` into callee-save), or (b) a C structure whose RTL doesn't trigger the NE-returns-1 path while preserving target's slt+bnez bytes (combinatorially constrained: the slt v0 + bnez v0 sequence inherently has condition `(ne v0 0)`, and any non-NE condition (subu+bltz etc.) emits different bytes). Strong evidence this function is at the C-source-axis structural ceiling for the lever class we have access to.

levers_tested.verdict: DIAGNOSTIC — pinpointed reorg.c relax_delay_slots as the flip pass; jump_insn 130 (ne 0 -> eq 0) + jump_insn 138 (label_ref 146 <-> 168) flipped between sched2 and dbr
DIAGNOSTIC — cc1psx produces SAME swap from same C; confirms NOT a fork-divergence per cc1psx-calibration-only policy
ALL score-1 variants converge on same 2-byte residual; V4/V5/V7 (dir<val test first) regress to 10/60-insns; V10/V11 (subu+bltz form) score 2 with different bytes
FOUND a score-0 form — `do { dispatch } while (0);` wrap. Cheat-reviewer FAIL (see rejected_forms below). Form preserved in rejected/.

remaining_gap: 2 byte diffs at masked insns 48 + 50: target emits `bnez v0, done; j next` (conditional to function-exit, fall-through to loop continuation), mine emits `beqz v0, next; j done`. Semantically equivalent, GCC chose opposite branch sense. Root cause FULLY DIAGNOSED (session 5, 2026-06-04): reorg.c's `relax_delay_slots` (gcc-2.7.2/reorg.c:3829-3851) invokes `mostly_true_jump` on the conditional, which returns 1 via the `case NE: return 1` heuristic at line 1407, triggering the swap. The only pre-emptive returns from mostly_true_jump that would give -1 are LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P (requires loop.c to recognize an enclosing loop), NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG, or rare_dest delta — all of which require either a real C loop construct (which triggers LICM hoist regression) or a do-while(0)-style coercion (cheat per cheat-reviewer 2026-06-04). cc1psx parity test confirms this is not a fork issue.
next_hypotheses: Build instrumented cc1 with BB2_REORG_DEBUG hooks ON reorg.c:3829-3851's swap path and BB2_JUMP_DEBUG on jump.c invert_jump — dump the EXACT C-source-driven RTL state that target's compilation produced (vs ours). Compare against a matched sibling in the same file with similar shape to identify what makes target's reorg take a different path.

Find C source that produces `subu+bltz` form instead of `slt+bnez` — the LT-against-0 condition returns 0 from mostly_true_jump and avoids the swap. The catch: target has slt+bnez bytes, so this form needs combine to FOLD subu+bltz back to slt+bnez (under combine's reach for `(lt (minus A B) 0)`). Worth testing combine's actual behavior on this RTL pattern (one-shot probe).

Cross-reference matched code6cac_b.c siblings — find one with the `if (cond) goto X; goto Y;` pattern in pure-C that DID match. Inspect what its C source did that target's compilation kept unaltered. m2c-reconstruct the target's specific RTL to see the original C statement order/types.

Inspect Kengo PS2 source nm_cpu/ files for the function — Kengo has only stubs in our extracted form, but the source MAY be available elsewhere as a cross-reference (the function name is preserved by the rename pass).

rejected_forms.name: do_while_loop
rejected_forms.reason: do { ... } while (a1val != 0) emits +4 insns (entry-test prologue); regresses to 67 insns vs 63

rejected_forms.name: inverted_outer_if
rejected_forms.reason: Inverting the outer (val != dir) test reshuffles BB layout and adds 5 more diffs

rejected_forms.name: while1_structured_loop
rejected_forms.reason: while(1) wrapping the body emits +4 entry-test insns; same shape as do/while regression, score 19 vs 1

rejected_forms.name: inner_inversion_goto_swap
rejected_forms.reason: Inner-inversion `if (dir >= val) goto next; goto done;` (swap which goto is conditional) — GCC canonicalizes >= and < to identical RTL post-combine; no effect on branch sense

rejected_forms.name: structured_else_if_chain
rejected_forms.reason: Nested if/else-if chain `if (val==dir) {...} else if (dir<val) goto done; goto next;` collapses to identical RTL/CFG as the 2-goto form; no effect

rejected_forms.name: do_while_zero_wrapping
rejected_forms.reason: PERMUTER FIND (score 0, full byte-match). Wraps the dispatch block in `do { ... } while (0);` to trigger NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG -> loop.c sets LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P on done -> mostly_true_jump returns -1 -> reorg's swap suppressed. Cheat-reviewer 2026-06-04 verdict FAIL: the do-while-0 wrap has zero semantic purpose (body executes once unconditionally), a human programmer would not write this, the justification is purely GCC-internal (NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG -> LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P -> mostly_true_jump -> swap suppression), and the intent is identical to the original `asm volatile("")` barrier (FORBIDDEN cheat-asm) with only the spelling changed. Same family as the existing find_empty_do_while_zero detector (engine/volatile_cheats.py:362) but with non-empty body. Form preserved at rejected/do_while_zero_wrapping.c. Suggested detector extension: flag `do { <any> } while (0);` when (a) body contains goto exiting the do-while AND (b) function has a goto-loop sibling (wrap is purely for marker injection).
